# Indirect Tensile Strength Test

**Summary**: A test to determine the tensile strength of a rock sample using a Brazilian Disk.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[2014-al-ameri|AL-AMERI, 2014]] used indirect tensile strength tests to evaluate the fracture resistance of carbonate rocks.

## Scientific Context

- [[2014-al-ameri|AL-AMERI, 2014]] Tensile strength is critical for understanding fracture propagation.

## Measurement and Characterization Methods

- [[2014-al-ameri|AL-AMERI, 2014]] measured using the Brazilian Disk method.

## Key Findings Across Sources

- The ITS test was used as a practical method to estimate the strength of core samples. ([[2014-al-ameri|AL-AMERI, 2014, p. 73]])
